name: Headless WordPress + Astro
title: WordPress + Astro
summary: A static editorial blog whose posts are pulled from a public WordPress REST API at build time and rendered to flat HTML with Astro — headless WordPress, no server at request time.
setup_questions: What's the WordPress site to pull from (the REST API base, e.g. https://wordpress.org/news/wp-json/wp/v2)?
What should the blog be called?
Which categories, authors, or content types should be featured or excluded?
What's the accent color (a hex like
sections: Header strip explaining 'Content from WordPress, rendered statically with Astro, hosted on Spacefast' with a source link
Blog index: full-width lead story plus a responsive grid of post cards (image, tags, title, excerpt, author, date, reading time)
Featured-image handling with a tasteful placeholder for posts that have none
Per-post static pages with title, byline, hero image, and the post's rendered HTML as editorial prose
Footer crediting the WordPress REST API source, Astro, and Spacefast
based_on: A real headless-CMS setup: WordPress as the editor, Astro as the static front end, pulling posts from the public WordPress REST API at build time (demo source: wordpress.org/news).
why_good: The canonical 'real developer integration' example — proves an agent can wire a live REST API into a static build, normalize messy CMS data, and ship a polished blog with zero backend at request time.
